stem: A candidate enters the hiring pipeline. The probability of passing resume screening is between 72% and 90% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing phone interview is 81%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing onsite interview is 74%. What is the overall probability that the candidate is hired?

ground_truth.answer: P(hired) ranges from 0.432 to 0.539 as the resume screening pass rate varies from 72% to 90%. Point estimate: 0.486.

solution_steps: The candidate must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the resume screening stage creates a 10.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the resume screening rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
